# Page Title: Mechanical Engineering | IIT Jammu

### Department Vision

The Mechanical Engineering Department of IIT Jammu was
established in 2016 and is one of the three founding departments of the Institute.
The department strives to play a role in nation-building by training engineers in an
effort to transform them into future leaders. We conduct translational research to
enable us to surmount technological barriers so that our present and future needs
are addressed. The department offers B.Tech., M.Tech., and Ph.D. programs. It
currently has 19 faculty members, 6 staff members, and approximately 150
undergraduate students (I – IV year), 28 postgraduate students (I – II
year students of Thermal and Energy Systems Engineering), and 38 Ph.D. students.

fundamental disciplines. A mechanical engineer is well-versed in the basic

#### Abhay Sharma

An academic and research leader with over 20 years of distinguished service across premier institutions, including **IIT Jammu**, **IIT Hyderabad**, and **KU Leuven (QS World Rank #42)**and Industry. Adept at institution building from the ground up, policy development, strategic planning, and academic governance, with transformative roles as **Dean**, **National Coordinator**, and **Head of Section**.  Authored over 100 scientific publications in Journals of high impact factor and conferences of international repute and led multi-crore research and infrastructure projects. An advocate and practitioner for **NEP 2020**, **interdisciplinary innovation**, and **global academic collaboration**

#### Arvind Kumar Rajput

**Dr. Arvind Kumar Rajput** is a faculty member in the Department of Mechanical Engineering specializing in Tribology, Fluid Film Bearings, and Advanced Lubrication Systems. His research focuses on hydrodynamic and hybrid journal bearings, porous and gas-lubricated bearings  functioing with advanced lubricants.  Dr. Rajput teaches Tribology, Solid Mechanics and Machine Design, and is a recipient of the Teaching Appreciation Award for his academic contributions for the academic year 2019-21. He also serves as a Board of Studies (BOS) Member at Government College of Engineering and Technology Jammu.

##### Research Interest

Tribological Design of Machine elements: Hydrodynamic/ hydrostatic  journal and thrust bearing), Tribological aspects in Wind turbine, TENG

#### Samrat Rao

Thank you for visiting my webpage. I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu. My research interests are in the simulation and analysis of the fluid dynamics of heated and unheated turbulent plumes which are an ansatz of clouds and forest-fires. I am also collaborating with my colleagues in the domain of heat-transfer in the materials science domain. My core teaching interest is in the intellectually challenging field of thermo-fluids.

I have received my undergraduate (BE) degree from NMAMIT Mangalore in 2004, graduate degree (ME) from IIEST Shibpur in 2007 and doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2014; all of them are in Mechanical Engineering.

Please do drop an email if you are interested in fulfilling your higher education ambitions with me.

##### Education Qualification

- PhD (2014), Department of Mech

Rocket engine exhaust is cooled by injecting water in very high quantities to suppress the acoustic noise levels and to reduce the exhaust gas temperature. Physical models of the atomization processes and experimental databases are currently not available in the case of free supersonic crossflow configurations.
